caption.full:
Ian Somerhalder hospitalized after funding $6M V Wars reshoots

Ian Somerhalder attempted to prevent his Netflix series V Wars from airing, even offering to return millions to the studio. He and partner James Gibb self-funded around $6 million for reshoots. The demanding pace led to Somerhalder experiencing a 'full body shutdown' and hospitalization.

What’s your take: principled exit or costly gamble?

AI-assisted recap by The Screen Report.

#MovieNews #VWars #IanSomerhalder #JamesGibb
caption.firstComment: Ian put his money where his principles stood — do you think stepping away was the right call or would you have fought differently?

facebook.text: Ian Somerhalder ended up in the hospital after self-funding $6 million in reshoots for his Netflix series V Wars. He offered to return millions to the studio to avoid putting his name on the initial cut, which he found unwatchable. The show was ultimately canceled by Netflix. What are your thoughts on this situation?

youtube.title: Ian Somerhalder Hospitalized After $6M V Wars Reshoots
youtube.description:
Ian Somerhalder was hospitalized after personally funding $6 million in reshoots for his Netflix series V Wars. He stated he didn't want to put his name on the initial cut, leading him and partner James Gibb to raise funds for new footage. The show was later canceled by Netflix.

AI-assisted recap by The Screen Report.

Watch more: https://thescreenreport.com/celebrity/why-ian-somerhalder-pulled-the-plug-on-acting-after-netflix-s-v-wars/
